items.title: Fitzroy River plan sparks concern among pastoralists and traditional owners
items.summary: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

items.title: US negotiator in 2015 Iran nuclear deal says Donald Trump 'delusional' on nuclear and regime change
items.summary: Robert Malley, who negotiated the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran, says Donald Trump's address to the US this week was designed to deceive.

items.title: Australia scores nine-wicket win over West Indies in final ODI in St Kitts
items.summary: Alana King stars with the ball, as the Australian women's team finish their white-ball tour of West Indies with a resounding nine-wicket win in St Kitts.

items.title: Port Macquarie family travels overseas for unproven stem-cell treatment as experts urge caution
items.summary: A young mother who travelled overseas for controversial stem cell therapy hopes new treatments will one day be available in Australia, but experts warn there's still a "long way" to go.

items.title: Cafe owners calls for visa changes to stop backpackers leaving WA's holiday hotspots

items.summary: Break out the chocolate, pick up the remote: we've got you covered for Easter weekend.

items.summary: Cameron Green is unable to arrest his shaky start to the Indian Premier League season, but Travis Head blasts back to his best.

items.title: WA holiday-makers stick closer to home over fuel supply fears

items.title: SA fishers demand urgent action to protect stocks, fearing long-term wipeout
items.summary: Easter is a popular time for consumers to purchase fish, but commercial fishers in South Australia say stocks have been decimated by the algal bloom. They fear it will get worse without further action.

items.title: Invasive feral hog deer discovered in New South Wales but authorities confident of eradication
items.summary: Standing only 70 centimetres tall, the hog deer has not been seen in the state for more than a decade, but poses a significant environmental threat.

items.title: Saints' Lance Collard to face tribunal for alleged homophobic slur in VFL match

items.title: Australia sources most of its jet fuel from Asia, but suppliers are feeling the pinch
items.summary: Australia imports most of its jet fuel from Asia, particularly China, Singapore and South Korea. But those countries rely on the Middle East for crude oil, meaning they are particularly vulnerable to the impacts of war.

items.summary: The seat of Narungga was so close it required a recount, and has now been won by One Nation candidate Chantelle Thomas by a margin of 58 votes over the Liberals.

items.summary: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

items.summary: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

items.title: Sam Kerr in, Mary Fowler not part of Matildas squad for FIFA Series in Kenya
items.summary: Sam Kerr has been named, but the Matildas will be missing a number of their other big guns when they travel to Kenya for the team's maiden FIFA Series in April.

items.title: Your Good Friday fish and chips may be coming from as far away as South Africa this Easter
items.summary: This Easter is the first in Western Australia under tough new demersal fishing rules. Seafood retailers say the change in supply and price is noticeable.

items.title: Australians continue to transit through Middle East despite 'do not travel' warnings

items.title: Albanese unveils long-awaited response to landmark gambling reform report

items.title: States and territories agree to further fuel excise cut, using extra GST revenue
items.summary: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

items.title: Australia to join 34 countries — but not the US — in meeting on Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Australia will join a virtual meeting of 35 countries to discuss plans to reopen and secure the Strait of Hormuz, and ease the oil shock rocking global markets.

items.title: More SA general practices charging extra administration fees to cover payroll tax

items.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued
items.summary: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

items.title: Crypto exchange platform markets in Australia despite ASIC intervention
items.summary: A major cryptocurrency platform is operating like "gambling" in Australia, according to experts, despite new regulatory laws.

items.title: Relocating tiger sharks may reduce short-term risk of shark bites, research suggests
items.summary: A new study analysing the behaviour of tiger sharks relocated from the Great Barrier Reef Marine Park under the Queensland Shark Control Program shows tiger sharks relocated from swimming beaches generally return to a beach within about three months.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie review: The only way out is through
